title: FT.EXPLAIN
description: Returns the execution plan for a complex query

go-redis.signature: FTExplain(ctx context.Context, index string, query string)

node_redis.signature: EXPLAIN(index: RedisArgument, query: RedisArgument, options?: FTSearchOptions)

nredisstack_sync.signature: string Explain(string indexName, string query, int? dialect = null)

nredisstack_async.signature: Task<string> ExplainAsync(string indexName, string query, int? dialect = null)

content:
Return the execution plan for a complex query [Examples](#examples) ## Required arguments
index is index name. You must first create the index using [`FT.CREATE`]({{< relref "commands/ft.create/" >}}).
query is query string, as if sent to FT.SEARCH`.
## Optional arguments
DIALECT {dialect_version} is dialect version under which to execute the query. If not specified, the query executes under the default dialect version set during module initial loading or via [`FT.CONFIG SET`]({{< relref "commands/ft.config-set/" >}}) command.
{{% alert title="Notes" color="warning" %}} - In the returned response, a `+` on a term is an indication of stemming. - Use `redis-cli --raw` to properly read line-breaks in the returned response. {{% /alert %}} ## Examples
Return the execution plan for a complex query {{< highlight bash >}} $ redis-cli --raw 127.0.0.1:6379> FT.EXPLAIN rd "(foo bar)|(hello world) @date:[100 200]|@date:[500 +inf]" INTERSECT { UNION { INTERSECT { foo bar } INTERSECT { hello world } } UNION { NUMERIC {100.000000 <= x <= 200.000000} NUMERIC {500.000000 <= x <= inf} } } {{< / highlight >}}
## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis Cloud
Flexible & Annual | Redis Cloud
Free & Fixed | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="ft-explain-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} One of the following: * [Bulk string]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings" >}}) containing the query execution plan. * [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index, syntax error in query. -tab-sep- One of the following: * [Bulk string]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings" >}}) containing the query execution plan. * [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index, syntax error in query. {{< /multitabs >}} ## See also [`FT.CREATE`]({{< relref "commands/ft.create/" >}}) | [`FT.SEARCH`]({{< relref "commands/ft.search/" >}}) | [`FT.CONFIG SET`]({{< relref "commands/ft.config-set/" >}}) ## Related topics [RediSearch]({{< relref "/develop/ai/search-and-query/" >}})

[Examples](#examples)

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Return the execution plan for a complex query

[Examples](#examples)

sections.title: Required arguments
sections.content:
index is index name. You must first create the index using [`FT.CREATE`]({{< relref "commands/ft.create/" >}}).
query is query string, as if sent to FT.SEARCH`.

sections.title: Optional arguments
sections.content:
DIALECT {dialect_version} is dialect version under which to execute the query. If not specified, the query executes under the default dialect version set during module initial loading or via [`FT.CONFIG SET`]({{< relref "commands/ft.config-set/" >}}) command.
{{% alert title="Notes" color="warning" %}} - In the returned response, a `+` on a term is an indication of stemming. - Use `redis-cli --raw` to properly read line-breaks in the returned response. {{% /alert %}}

sections.title: Examples
sections.content: Return the execution plan for a complex query {{< highlight bash >}} $ redis-cli --raw 127.0.0.1:6379> FT.EXPLAIN rd "(foo bar)|(hello world) @date:[100 200]|@date:[500 +inf]" INTERSECT { UNION { INTERSECT { foo bar } INTERSECT { hello world } } UNION { NUMERIC {100.000000 <= x <= 200.000000} NUMERIC {500.000000 <= x <= inf} } } {{< / highlight >}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis Cloud
Flexible & Annual | Redis Cloud
Free & Fixed | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | ✅ Supported | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
{{< multitabs id="ft-explain-return-info" 
    tab1="RESP2" 
    tab2="RESP3" >}}

One of the following:
* [Bulk string]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings" >}}) containing the query execution plan.
* [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index, syntax error in query.

-tab-sep-

One of the following:
* [Bulk string]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#bulk-strings" >}}) containing the query execution plan.
* [Simple error reply]({{< relref "/develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-errors" >}}) in these cases: no such index, syntax error in query.

{{< /multitabs >}}
